Fig. 1: Overview of the training process in MILE. Using the input images Xt, the domain prototype Pt is computed using a frozen pretrained network. LoRA expert Dt is instantiated and trained while keeping the base network frozen.
Fig. 5: Qualitative comparison of segmentation results for domain-incremental learning of geographical domains using Cityscapes [2], BDD [30], and IDD [27].
Table 1: Results (mIoU) on the sequence CSday → ACDCfog → ACDCrain → ACDCsnow → ACDCnight after learning all tasks.
